content.problemStatement:
The country of Siruseri has A×BA \times BA×B districts. You want to create AAA states from these districts, such that each state has exactly BBB districts, and each district is part of exactly one state. You don't care about the geographical location of the districts. You can pick any BBB districts and make it into a state.

There are only two parties contesting in the coming elections: P1P_1P1​ and P2P_2P2​. You know the number of votes that each party receives in each district. In the i-th district, P1P_1P1​ gets cic_ici​ votes and P2P_2P2​ gets did_idi​ votes. You are guaranteed that all these 2×A×B2 \times A \times B2×A×B integers (the number of votes received by each party in the districts) are distinct. Also, both AAA and BBB are odd.

Suppose you have chosen which districts belong to which states, then, to find out who wins any particular state, they follow a weird rule: Suppose the number of votes that P1P_1P1​ gets in the BBB districts of a particular state are x1,x2,…,xBx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Bx1​,x2​,…,xB​, and the number of votes that P2P_2P2​ gets in the BBB districts of this state are y1,y2,…,yBy_1, y_2, \ldots, y_By1​,y2​,…,yB​. Then among all these 2×B2 \times B2×B numbers, the largest number is chosen (note that we are guaranteed of an unique largest number). If that number is some xix_ixi​, then P1P_1P1​ wins this state. If the largest number is some yjy_jyj​, then P2P_2P2​ wins this state.

You secretly support the party P1P_1P1​, and hence you want to assign the districts to states, in such a way, that the number of states won by P1P_1P1​ is maximized. Find this maximum number of states that P1P_1P1​ can win.

Note that cic_ici​ and did_idi​ will always remain associated with the i-th district. If the i-th district gets assigned to a particular state, then both cic_ici​ and did_idi​ will be considered when deciding who won that state.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer, TTT, the number of testcases. The description of each testcase follows.
The first line of each testcase contains two integers, AAA and BBB.
The second line of each testcase contains A×BA \times BA×B integers: c1,c2,…,cA×Bc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_{A \times B}c1​,c2​,…,cA×B​, the number of votes won by P1P_1P1​ in the districts.
The third line of each testcase contains A×BA \times BA×B integers: d1,d2,…,dA×Bd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_{A \times B}d1​,d2​,…,dA×B​, the number of votes won by P2P_2P2​ in the districts.


Output Format
For each testcase output a single line which contains the maximum number of states that P1P_1P1​ can win.
constraints: All the ci and di will be distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer, T, the number of testcases. The description of each testcase follows. The first line of each testcase contains two integers, A and B. The second line of each testcase contains A×B integers: c1,c2,…,cA×B, the number of votes won by P1 in the districts. The third line of each testcase contains A×B integers: d1,d2,…,dA×B, the number of votes won by P2 in the districts.
content.outputFormat: For each testcase output a single line which contains the maximum number of states that P1 can win.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: Since you have to form only 1 state, there is no choice, but to put all the 3 districts in that same state. Now to figure out who wins that single state, we take the maximum among {4, 2, 9, 5, 6, 7}. The maximum is 9, and that belongs to P1. Hence P1 wins this state. And because they have won 1 state, the answer is 1.
Testcase 2: Similarly, there is no choice here. To figure out who wins that single state, we take the maximum among {4, 2, 9, 5, 10, 7}. The maximum is 10, and that belongs to P2. Hence P2 wins this state. And because P1 have won no states, the answer is 0.
Testcase 3: We need to make three states with three districts each. Suppose we that the 3rd, 5th and 7th districts and form a state, the votes in them would be {11, 16, 15, 19, 20, 12}. The max among these is 20, and that belongs to P1. Hence P1 would win this state.
Similarly, suppose we make the second state with the 2nd, 4th and 8th districts, the votes in them would be {14, 13, 4, 9, 1, 8}. The max among these is 14, and that belongs to P1. Hence P1 would win this state.
The remaining three districts: 1st, 6th and 9th districts form the third state. The votes in them would be {7, 2, 5, 6, 17, 10}. The max among these is 17, and that belongs to P1. Hence P1 would win this state.
In this situation, P1 wins three states. You obviously cannot do any better. Hence the answer is 3.

solution.approach: To maximize the number of states that P1 can win, we need to assign the districts to states such that the maximum number of votes in each state belongs to P1. We can achieve this by sorting the districts based on the maximum number of votes they can get for P1 and then assigning them to states in a greedy manner.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * A * B * log(A * B)) — due to sorting the districts for each testcase
solution.spaceComplexity: O(A * B) — for storing the districts and their corresponding votes

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    A, B = map(int, input().split())
    c = list(map(int, input().split()))
    d = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_votes = [max(c[i], d[i]) for i in range(A * B)]
    max_votes.sort(reverse=True)
    count = 0
    for i in range(0, A * B, B):
        max_val = max(c[i:i+B])
        if max_val == max_votes[i // B]:
            count += 1
    print(count)
